title: Sweet Potato Curry
description: A hearty and flavorful curry with sweet potatoes, quinoa, and lentils in a coconut milk base.
ingredients: 1 (15 ounce) can of beans (black, pinto, etc) [optional]
instructions: Add all ingredients to a large pot and stir until combined. Bring mixture to a boil, reduce to a simmer and simmer covered until liquid has absorbed and quinoa, lentils and sweet potato are cooked through, 50-60 minutes.

You will want to uncover the pot and give it a good stir every so often to make sure nothing is sticking to the bottom and that there is enough liquid. If for some reason you run out of liquid, you can add in a little bit more broth, or water.
Garnish with chopped cashews, fresh cilantro, or pickled red onions, if desired.
Add-ons: If you want to make it more hearty, you can add a can of beans (black, pinto, etc), some tempeh cut into bite sized pieces, TVP, or pea crumbles.

title: Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: Soft and chewy chocolate chip cookies that are perfect for any occasion.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a small bowl, mix flour, baking soda, and salt.
In a large bowl, cream together butter and both sugars until fluffy.
Beat in eggs one at a time, then stir in vanilla.
Gradually blend in the flour mixture.
Stir in chocolate chips.
Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto ungreased cookie sheets.
Bake for 9 to 11 minutes or until golden brown.
Cool on baking sheet for 2 minutes before removing to a wire rack.

title: Authentic Spaghetti Carbonara
description: Classic Italian pasta dish with eggs, cheese, and pancetta.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil and cook spaghetti according to package directions.
While pasta cooks, cook pancetta in a large skillet until crispy, about 8-10 minutes.
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, Pecorino Romano, Parmesan, and plenty of black pepper.
When pasta is al dente, reserve 1 cup pasta water, then drain.
Add hot pasta to the skillet with pancetta (heat off).
Pour egg mixture over pasta and toss quickly, adding pasta water as needed to create a creamy sauce.
Serve immediately with extra cheese and black pepper.

title: Quick Chicken Stir Fry
description: A fast and healthy weeknight dinner with vegetables and tender chicken.
ingredients: 1 lb boneless chicken breast, cut into bite-sized pieces
instructions: Marinate chicken in soy sauce and cornstarch for 10 minutes.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat.
Add chicken and stir-fry until golden and cooked through, about 5-6 minutes.
Remove chicken and set aside.
Add bell pepper and broccoli to the same skillet, stir-frying for 3-4 minutes.
Add garlic and ginger, cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return chicken to the skillet along with chicken broth and oyster sauce.
Stir everything together and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Drizzle with sesame oil and serve over rice.

title: Moist Banana Bread
description: A classic banana bread recipe that's perfect for using upripe bananas.
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9x5 inch loaf pan.
In a large bowl, mash the bananas with a fork until smooth.
Stir melted butter into the mashed bananas.
Mix in baking soda and salt.
Stir in sugar, beaten egg, and vanilla extract.
Mix in flour until just combined.
Pour batter into prepared loaf pan.
Bake for 60-65 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into center comes out clean.
Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack to cool completely.

title: Traditional Greek Salad
description: Fresh and colorful Mediterranean salad with feta cheese and olives.
ingredients: 1 green bell pepper, cut into rings
instructions: Cut tomatoes into wedges and place in a large bowl.
Slice cucumber and add to the bowl.
Thinly slice red onion and add to vegetables.
Cut green bell pepper into rings and add to the salad.
Add Kalamata olives to the bowl.
Gently fold in cubed feta cheese.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, red wine vinegar, and oregano.
Pour dressing over salad and toss gently.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Let salad sit for 15 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld.

title: Harissa Chickpeas
description: A flavorful and spicy North African dish with chickpeas in a rich harissa sauce.
ingredients: 1 teaspoon salt or more to taste, divided
1 (15 ounce) can of chickpeas drained or 1.5 cups cooked chickpeas or other white beans
.5 teaspoon salt or more to taste, divided
1 tablespoon garlic paste or 4 cloves of garlic, minced
2 cups (80 g) of chopped leafy greens of choice such as Swiss chard, spinach, mustard greens, etc
1 cup (236.59 ml) full fat coconut milk or coconut cream (use the entire can if you like it more saucy)
1 teaspoon lemon juice, cilantro, and black pepper, for garnish
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Add the oil and once it's hot, add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is translucent and starting to get golden on some outer edges, about 7 to 9 minutes.
Add in the garlic, smoked paprika, harissa, and cumin and mix really well to coat the onion. Cook for another minute so that the garlic gets cooked.
Then add in the harissa paste and mix well to coat everything. Cook for another minute.
Add in the chickpeas (drained or cooked) and the vegetable broth. Bring to a simmer and cook, partially covered, for 20 to 25 minutes, or until the chickpeas are tender but not falling apart.

Stir in the coconut milk (or cream) and lemon juice. If you want more sauce, you can add in a tablespoon or two of non-dairy yogurt or sour cream. Add in a good squeeze of lemon juice and adjust salt and heat as needed.
Garnish with cilantro or parsley along with some black pepper and some more lemon juice. Serve with flatbread or rice or with some sourdough toast or garlic bread or whatever else that you like.

title: Quick Pickled Red Onions
description: Simple and tangy pickled red onions perfect for sandwiches and salads.
ingredients: .67 tablespoons salt per cup of pickling liquid
instructions: Cut the red onion and transfer into a heat safe jar with a lid. I typically make half moons with it, and cut them in half so they're shorter.
Mix your pickling liquid, add in any desired flavor customizations (peppercorns, etc), pour it into a small saucepan and bring to a boil.
Pour the pickling liquid into the jar, filling it up and ensuring the onion is submerged. You may want to press it down from the opening with a measuring spoon to ensure all the onion is submerged.

Let cool on the counter, and store in the fridge afterwards. You can use them pretty soon afterwards, once they're more pink than red (~15 mins), but waiting an hour or more may help the flavor develop more.

title: Kung Pao Chickpeas
description: A flavorful Asian-inspired dish with chickpeas in a savory Kung Pao sauce.
ingredients: 3 cups cooked chickpeas (or two 15 oz. cans, rinsed and drained)
2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar (the more syrupy, the better)
.5 teaspoon garlic powder (or 2 minced garlic cloves)
.5 teaspoon ground ginger (or 2 teaspoons fresh minced ginger)
cooked white rice, for serving (or cauliflower rice)
instructions: Add red onion, bell pepper, and chickpeas into bowl of your slow cooker.
In a small bowl, whisk together tamari, vinegar, maple syrup, garlic, ginger, red pepper flakes, and sesame oil. Pour sauce into slow cooker and stir well.

Cover slow cooker and set it to cook for 3 hours on high heat, or 6 hours on low heat. If you need to cook it longer due to your schedule, I'd recommend adding 2-4 tablespoons of water to mixture to help prevent sauce from over-cooking.
Once cooking is done, give chickpeas a stir and serve them warm over a bed of white rice (or cauliflower rice) with a sprinkling of green onions and sesame seeds on top.
Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the fridge for up to a week.

title: Sweet Potato Curry
description: A flavorful and comforting curry with roasted sweet potatoes and a blend of warming spices.
ingredients: 2 large sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Toss sweet potatoes with 1 tablespoon oil, salt, and pepper on a baking sheet. Roast for 20-25 minutes, or until tender.
In a large pan, heat 1 tablespoon oil over medium heat. Add onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to brown, about 8 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, cumin, curry powder, turmeric, and cayenne pepper to the pan. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes.
Stir in coconut milk and vegetable broth. Bring to a simmer.
Add roasted sweet potatoes to the pan and stir to combine. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped cilantro if desired.
